publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-04-07 23:45:17 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, Manuscripts, Newspapers, Printed Materials, Publications, Scrapbooks
did.abstract: Correspondence and scrapbooks of newspaper clippings and memorabilia, primarily concerned with Sulzer's political activities.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: William Sulzer was a lawyer, a U.S. Representative from New York, 1895-1912, and governor of New York from January 1 to October 18, 1913, when he was removed from office.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: William Sulzer papers, #1147. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
This file contains the information contained here, but also some additional notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and scrapbooks of newspaper clippings and memorabilia, primarily concerned with Sulzer's political activities. The material relates to Sulzer's campaign for direct primary elections for New York State and his impeachment and subsequent removal from the office of Governor. Includes letters from government officials, organizations, and private citizens giving their views and discussing action taken on these two matters. Also letters concerning such matters as the Governors' Conference, good roads, canal navigation, subway contracts, public institutions, petitions, and recommendations for various bills and resolutions, grievances, and complaints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also includes letters to Sulzer from Sun Yat-sen: one from Shangahi, 1916, expressing a desire to have the International Settlement in Shanghai and to institute reforms in city government in China; from Canton, 1918, concerning the current constitutional crisis in China and soliciting Sulzer's support; and a letter from Austin P. Snow, Washington, D.C., 1912, containing a copy of a letter from Sun Yat-sen concerning the recognition of the Chinese Republic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of "Watchman, What of the Night" (bound tract of the American Party), autographed by Governor Sulzer, September 1, 1916.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also, an autograph book, (with signatures of such people as Edwin Markham, Vincent Astor, Thornton W. Burgess, John Dewey, Margaret Woodrow Wilson, and Harry Lauder), a letter (March 21, 1928) from Sulzer to M/M Thomas W. Evans, an essay by Sulzer, entitled, "Of Time and Space" and a few newspaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Additional correspondence, 1917-1932; political pamphlets; memos; and miscellany

unittitle.extref: Link to the finding aid. 20 MB PDF file.

did.unittitle: State of New York, Court for the Trial of Impeachment
State of New York, Court for the Trial of Impeachment
State of New York, Court for the Trial of Impeachment
State of New York, Court for the Trial of Impeachment
State of New York, Court for the Trial of Impeachment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items are arranged between two boxes. Includes volumes 17-35, 53-86.

did.unittitle: Consolidated laws, International Law Digest, Briefs, Homicide, Penal Codes, Interviews with journalists
Interviews with journalists; Correspondence A-B; (Personal Clippings 1913-14)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes volumes 7, 47-50 (Personal Clippings 1913-1914) and volume 51 (Personal Clippings 1910).

did.unittitle: Clippings, letters, and autograph guest book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes volumes 8, 87-119. Autograph guest book contains Roosevelt amongst many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On microfilm--original at State Historical Society of Wisconsin. Contains correspondence of William Sulver, attorney, New York State and National Legislator, and New York State Governor. Includes miscellaneous materials pertaining to the American Party. (1 negative reel)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by American Party in support of Sulver. (55 pp.)

did.unittitle: Copy of letter from W.S. to William Church Osborn, Chairman of the Democratic State Committee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: W.S. asks Osborn to support a genuine direct primary law (which he says Osborn had previously refused to do), by using his influence to introduce such a law in the extraordinary session of the legislature. W.S. outlines his own proposed amendments to the inadequate direct primary law passed in 1913, and also asks Osborn to move up the 1914 Primary Registration day, which falls of the Jewish Day of Atonement so "so hundreds of thousands of Jewish citizens shall not be disenfranchised."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter states that his position has been in favor of additional legislation for the present direct primary law.

did.unittitle: News Clipping re: "National Memorial to Thomas Paine", by Olin J. Ross of the Columbus, Ohio, Bar

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally a speech given by Ross on January 29, 1923.

did.unittitle: Elsie Hess to W.S.; Merrick, Long Island, NY
L.E. Whicher (Pres. Cori Gold Mines Co.; NY) to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: re: W.S. depositing his stock in escrow.

did.unittitle: Copy of letter from W.S. to Hon. E. Henry Lacombe, U.S. Circuit Judge; Albany, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter thanking L. for his political support, and commenting that "the Curtis story is merely a smoke screen of the bosses," and asserting "no threats shall deter me from…carrying out…my campaign pledges to the people."

did.unittitle: Typescript of a speech by W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: States he will again run as Governor of New York State. (4 pp., but other items occur between them on microfilm.)

did.unittitle: Pamphlet opposing repeal of direct primary law

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reprinted by Direct Primary Comm. Of N.Y. Consists of speech by W.S. at Catskill, N.Y., Courthouse, June 6, 1913.

did.unittitle: List of names of American Party "helpers."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 pp., but other items occur between them.

did.unittitle: List of names, home and business addresses of American Party candidates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Candidate electors for President, Vice-President, and N.Y.S. candidates for offices including Governor and legislators. 1 page.

did.unittitle: Reprint of letter to the editor of "Knickerbocker Press"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Knickerbocker Press, August 31, 1918. This letter is from George W. Dennison praises W.S. and criticises Tammany.

did.unittitle: Broadside (?) from American Party urging Charles E. Whitman for N.Y.S. Governor
Copy of letter from W.S. to George W. Beakley (?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The history of the Long Sault Charter, and describing his own opposition to it while Governor. 2 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issues by American Pary, N.Y. Includes a statement supporting Charles Evans for Pres., and Whitman for Gov., other items, many re Sulzer; and the American Party Platform for 1916. 47pp.

did.unittitle: Typescript of a speech by W.S.
Letter to W.S. from James H. Perkins, Pres. Farmer's Loan and Trust Co., N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re services the company can render Americans traveling in Europe

did.unittitle: Letter to W.S. from Frank E. Xavier, ed. Of "The Yonkers Herald"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re asking W.S. for his opinion condcerning the length of term and election time of N.Y.S. Governors.

did.unittitle: Copy of W.S.'s reply to Xavier
Letter circular from W.S. on Amercian Party Stationary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sent to enrolled Progressives, asking them to atten Progressive Primary that month and to vote for him as Gov., so that he can continue his reform work.

did.unittitle: Pamphlet-"Charter and Rules and By-Laws of the American Party"
Clay Cunningham, business manager for Lincoln Memorial University, to W.S.
Copy of letter from Sulzer to Cunningham
Copy of letter from W.S. to Cornell University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re offering to donate correspondence and books to C.U. Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Short statement signed by Sulver, N.Y.C.

did.unittitle: Letter from Saul S. Myers, N.Y. attorney, to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re Mosher and Wallace, Bankrupts. 4 typescript pp., but 4th is separated from the first 3 by other items.

did.unittitle: Pamphlet-"Swastik", subtitled "The Science of How to Get Rich"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Swastika Science Society, N.Y. Introduction states that the S.S.S. "is organized…[to educate] the people in Fundamental Economics…hence, How to Get Rich." Pamphlet compiled from newpaper clippings, one of them a May 1915 speech by W.S. on the economics of the liquor question; and other items including letters from Sulzer. Samuel Bell Thomas was Secretary of the S.S.S. 22pp.

did.unittitle: Letter from George B. Roane, President of the Templar Knights Commanders Assoc., to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re an invitation to W.S. to speak for the group

did.unittitle: Copy of letter from W.S. to Hon. Frederick M. Davenport, Clinton, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Davenport was the other candidate besides W.S. for the Progressive's nomination for Governor in the primary. Sulzer suggests debates between the two to decide who should receive Progressive support.

did.unittitle: W.M. Pixley, Seattle attorney, to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re W.S.'s mining and lumber interests.

did.unittitle: Letter from John McKee (Chairman) and A.C. Lasswell (Secretary) of Prohibition Party of N.Y. to Col. Theodore Roosevelt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter states the Prohibition Party and T.R. both desire to end boss rule in N.Y., and that the re-election of Sulzer, "the only genuine Progressive in this crisis," can accomplish this. They ask T.R. to help them insure Sulzer's election.

did.unittitle: Copy of letter from W.S. to Judge John Ford, N.Y.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re W.S. promises his help in passing Ford's bill against obscene literature.

did.unittitle: Copy of letter from W.S. to Olin J. Ross, Columbus, Ohio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re W.S. asking him to print his speech urging a national memorial for Thomas Paine, and praising Paine.

did.unittitle: Easter card to W.S. from Elsie Hess
Typescript copy of a part of W.S.'s speech at the Progressive Club, Brooklyn, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The People of N.Y.S. on the Relation of William Sulzer against William Sohmer, Comptroller of the State of N.Y., and related items, such as the petition of W.S., and affidavits. 49 pp., includes index.

did.unittitle: Letter to W.S. from "Sister Anne" (his sister?), Orange, N.J.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Apparently a 3 page letter but the order seems confused.

did.unittitle: Typescripts of pp. 2-4 of lists of W.S.'s records as a Progressive governor, state and national legislator

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Part of W.S.'s campaign to win Progressive support to himself rather than to Frederick Davenport.

did.unittitle: Letter to W.S. from Ernest Harvier, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Neither McAdoo, Ford, or Cox will be seriously considere by the Democrats. The man they will nominate, in all probability, is Senator Ralsont with Smith for Vice-President…Meanwhile, our Republican friends are slumbering at the political cross-roads."

did.unittitle: Edgar G. Jones, Baltimore, to W.S.
Percy Stickney Grant, Ascension Rectory, N.Y.C., to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A letter of thanks for W.S.'s friendship during the writer's difficulties, Lent.

did.unittitle: Rev. C.D. Ellett of the First Presbyterian Church, Manning, Iowa, to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Our friend Phillipps…is mistaken as to a new party. The American Party had a good start. It met its Waterloo at St. Paul. The 'dry' organization did it. We are now in the struggle for law enforcement. This responsibility rests upon both parties alike. The new anti-papal party organized recently in St. Louis is nearer the mark."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A column praising W.S. and stating that there is no doubt about his re-election to Congress. Published weekly by Nationalist Pub. Co., M.T. O'Reilly, Ed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re mining in Washington state area.

did.unittitle: W.S. to Chester C. Platt, Madison, Wisconsin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: States his friends organized "American Party" to bring about his election as New York State Governor; explains why they chose the Prohibition Party nomination rather than the Democrat or Independent; says he opposed the 18th Amendment and Volstead Act but would have supported a high excise tax; and criticizes the above laws.

did.unittitle: W.S. Edwards, Phoenix, Arizona, to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re some of W.S.'s financial transactions.

did.unittitle: W.S. Edwards, Phoenix, Arizona, to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re some of W.S.'s financial transactions.

did.unittitle: J. Martin McKee, Doorkeeper's Dept., U.S. House of Rep. to W.S.
Richard M. Hurd, Treasurer, Berkshire Industrial Farm, Inc., Canaan, N.Y. to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Circular letter asking for financial aid.

did.unittitle: George R. Hill, Phoenix, Arizona, attorney to W.S.
Photograph of Fourth Street, South from Main, in La Crosse, Wisconsin
Postcard from T.J. Henry (?) to W.S.
Part of a newspaper clipping concerning a speech by W.S. supporting proposed national memorial to Thomas Paine
Willis J. Abbot, Manager, Press Bureau of Democratic Nat'l Committee, Chicago, to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter denying he supports Pattison for Vice-President. Includes letter from Abbot, Dec., 10, no year.

did.unittitle: John P. Altgeld, Chicago, to Bernhard D. O'Connell, N.Y.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter states, "I have a very hgh opinion of Mr. Sulzer and think he is making a brilliant record in Congress. So far as the Vice-Presidency is concerned, I have given that no attention and am not in a situation to do anything about it."

did.unittitle: William V. Allen, Chairman of the U.S. Senate Committee on Forest Reservations and Protection of Game to W.S., Madison, Nebraska

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "…It is quite likely you will receive [Robinson's] vote for the Democratic leadership of the House…I think if you would see Stark, Sutherland, and Neville from this state, it would be of value to you."

did.unittitle: Letter to W.S. on Editorial Dept. stationary, Cosmopolitan Magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First page only, Jan., 1, no year.

did.unittitle: Willis J. Abbot, Ann Arbor, Michigan, to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentions his new job as manager of Press Bureau of Demo. Nat'l Committee and one of his policies, and comments, "I am glad you are making a fight on the money issue. I think it is sure to come up in the campaign and the action of the Democratic members who are acting with the Republicans will rise up to curse the party and them." Dec., 15, no year.

did.unittitle: Copy of a letter from W.S. to the Editor of the "New York World"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter suggests an editorial favoring a convention to revise the Federal Constitution, and mentions some revisions he considers fundamental.

did.unittitle: William V. Allen of Forest Reservation Committee of U.S. Senate to W.S.
Charles Frederic Adams, N.Y.C. attorney, to W.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Asks W.S. to support the "Ainsworth Bill" to give the N.Y.S. Counties "home rule" in taxation questions.

did.unittitle: Certificate of Incorporation of the American Party, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The directors were Samuel Bell Thomas, Alexander S. Bacon, Theodorus Van Wyck, Albert Bruchlos, Robert Abrahams, Samuel Friedman, Emil Kovarik, John W. Hughes, and Vito Contessa, all of N.Y.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issued by the Liberty League, N.Y.C. Includes a 7 pp. sketch of W.S. by Julius Chambers, and related items. 32 pp.

did.unittitle: Correspondence of public figures, Cornell employess and other notables

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items were noted during arrangement of the collection by Anne Warren between October 1963 and January 1964.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of letter from Chester C. Platt, editor of "Ithaca Dem", November 4, 1897. Typed letter, signed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May be to Theodore Roosevelt letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May be to Theodore Roosevelt letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May be to Theodore Roosevelt letters.

did.unittitle: Proceedings of the Board of Trustees
Stimson, Henry L. (Secretary of the Army)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with Willard Austen, Cornell University Librarian.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of correspondence with Willard Austen, Cornell University Librarian.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unordered and unsorted but box says 1913

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unordered and unsorted but box says 1913

scopecontent.p: Unordered and unsorted but box says 1913

scopecontent.p: Unordered and unsorted but box says 1913

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Following letters are to be found within, Correspondence (1913).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carbon copy of letter to Frawley Committee, re Gov. Sulver, signed by a "genuine Democrat".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes list of resolutions passed by Democratic Committee of Saratoga County.

did.unittitle: Godard, George S. (State Librarian, Connecticut State Library); Hartford, CT
Ilsen, Martin ("The German Herald"); NY, NY
Typewritten statement of the principles of Jeffersonian democracy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes card noting receipt of of Sulver speeches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clipping from German language newspaper; includes translation.
